FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Namespace FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite

Classes

DWriteItemizer

DirectWrite itemizer (text.md §4.4): segments text into BiDi × script runs and computes UAX #14 line-break opportunities via IDWriteTextAnalyzer. DirectWrite calls back into the hand-authored callee CCWs FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.TextAnalysisSourceCcw (supplies the text) and FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.TextAnalysisSinkCcw (collects the runs). COM bound via TerraFX raw pointers; the CCW vtables mirror the ComputeSharp PixelShaderEffect pattern. Single-thread-confined: Itemize(ReadOnlySpan<char>, List<ItemRun>, List<BreakOpp>) calls AnalyzeBidi/Script/LineBreakpoints serially on the caller thread.

DWriteTextShaper

DirectWrite glyph shaper (text.md §4.4): turns one itemized run (single face + script + direction) into positioned glyphs via IDWriteTextAnalyzer.GetGlyphs + GetGlyphPlacements — real cluster/ligature/kerning/RTL shaping, not per-char design advances. Advances come out in DIP (GetGlyphPlacements is given the DIP em size). Scratch buffers are grown and reused; Shape(char*, int, int, IDWriteFontFace*, float, ushort, byte, bool) returns a span into the reused output buffer (valid until the next call). COM via TerraFX raw pointers.

DirectWriteBackend

SCAFFOLD (the reference Windows text backend — see design/subsystems/text.md). Will implement the text seam over DirectWrite: itemize (BiDi/script/line-break) → font-fallback → shape, glyph rasterization into the GPU atlas, and the callee-side IDWriteTextAnalysisSource/Sink CCWs. Headless font stub backs the slice today.

DirectWriteFontSystem

The DirectWrite text backend (IFontSystem) — a thin wrapper over TextLayoutEngine. Every member (measurement AND the editor queries) runs the full itemize → shape → wrap/trim → position pipeline (kerning, ligatures, complex script, UAX #14 line breaking, CJK/emoji font fallback), so the measured box, the hit-tested caret and the selection rects all come from the SAME layout the renderer — which uses the same engine — actually draws. measure ≡ hit-test ≡ render: this parity is what keeps shaped glyphs from overlapping (the divergence that read as bold when the renderer was shaped but measurement was per-char). Touched on the UI thread during phase-6 layout and at edit/drag time; the render thread owns a separate engine instance, so queries here can never corrupt render-side buffers. Query results live in the engine's reused grow-only tables — 0 steady-state allocation per keystroke/pointer-move.

TextLayoutEngine

The DirectWrite layout engine (text.md §8): itemize → shape (per run) → wrap (UAX #14 break opportunities) → position, with BiDi L2 reordering per line. Drives BOTH measurement (metrics) and rendering (positioned glyphs) so they layout identically — and the editor queries (HitTest(Point2, out bool)/CaretAt(int, out float, out float, out float, out int)/RangeRects(int, int, Span<RectF>)) read the retained per-glyph cluster + per-line tables of the SAME layout, so hit-testing matches rendering exactly. Owns its itemizer + shaper + face cache; reusable, single-thread-confined. Results live in reused grow-only buffers (valid until the next Layout(ReadOnlySpan<char>, string, int, float, float, int, int, int, float, float, int, int, ReadOnlySpan<SpanStyle>, StringTable?); queries fire per keystroke/pointer-move with 0 steady-state allocation). v1: design-advance shaping with kerning/ligatures/complex-script; font fallback + color glyphs are layered on in later phases; query edge mapping is leading=left (LTR — BiDi-correct edges arrive with the RTL workstream, but the scan is already cluster-correct and walks the visual order).

Structs

LaidCluster

Source mapping for one laid glyph — the hit-test companion to LaidGlyph (parallel to Glyphs minus the synthetic trim ellipsis): the UTF-16 index of the cluster's first source char (taken from the shaper's cluster map, never re-derived from char counts), the glyph's leading pen X and advance on its line, and its line index. Entries are stored in the VISUAL order glyphs were placed (after BiDi L2 reordering), so an in-order scan walks the line left→right.

LaidGlyph

One laid-out glyph: post-shaping glyph id, the DWrite face it belongs to (as nint), and its pen position in DIP — X within the paragraph (line-relative origin folded in), Y = its line baseline from the top. Size is the DIP em size the glyph was shaped at (spans may override the base size) and Span the index of the SpanStyle it belongs to (−1 = the base style) — the renderer rasterizes at Size and tints per span (rtb-01 inline runs).

LaidLine

One laid-out line: the UTF-16 source range it covers — [StartChar, EndChar), where EndChar is the next line's StartChar (the text length on the last line) — its vertical band (Top/Height), its rendered advance width (including a trim ellipsis), and its window [FirstGlyph, FirstGlyph + GlyphCount) into Clusters. Chars dropped by trimming have no cluster entry; queries clamp them to the trimmed edge.

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
